metadata.source: AWS Control Guides from ControlCompass (via NIST 800-53 mapping)
metadata.description: AWS implementation guides for CMMC Level 2 practices

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Enable MFA for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users that have a console password
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Enable MFA for IAM users that have a console password in order to strengthen user authentication.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Ensure all AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user groups contain users
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Ensure that all IAM user groups contain users, or remove empty user groups when no longer needed so the groups cannot be used to unintentionally grant access.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) identities
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Manage access in AWS by creating and attaching IAM policies to ensure that only authorized individuals have access to data and resources.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Configure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Configure MFA for IAM users to protect resources from unauthorized access.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Configure least privilege access policies in AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Ensure that access policy permissions explicitly restrict the use of wildcards "*" and "allow all" in AWS IAM in order to enforce least privilege access and prevent unintended or unauthorized access to resources.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Block public access to Amazon S3 buckets
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Configure Amazon S3 to block public access to S3 buckets in order to protect data from unauthorized access.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Disable public access to Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) endpoints
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Disable public access to Amazon EKS endpoints in order to avoid unintentional exposure and access to the cluster.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Disable direct internet access for Amazon SageMaker Notebook instance
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Restrict SageMaker Notebook instances from having direct internet access to avoid potential unauthorized access and data exposure.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Prevent IAM inline policies from using wildcard permissions in both Action and Resource elements
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from including 'Effect' 'Allow' with 'Action' '*' over 'Resource' '*' to enforce least privilege access.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Enforce IAM password policy to require at least one uppercase letter
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Enforces that IAM password policies require at least one uppercase letter in passwords, strengthening authentication security against brute force attacks.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Enforce strong password policies for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Requires IAM user password policies to implement strong configurations, preventing the use of weak passwords and reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Enforce IAM password policy to require at least one lowercase letter
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Enforces that IAM password policies require at least one lowercase letter in passwords to strengthen authentication security.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Ensure IAM users do not have policies directly attached in AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Enforce IAM users to inherit permissions from IAM groups or roles rather than having inline or managed policies directly attached.

ac.l2-3.1.1.title: Restrict IAM inline policies from using wildcard service actions in AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
ac.l2-3.1.1.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from using 'Effect' 'Allow' with wildcard service actions to enforce least privilege access.

ac.l2-3.1.2.title: Configure least privilege access policies in AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
ac.l2-3.1.2.description: Ensure that access policy permissions explicitly restrict the use of wildcards "*" and "allow all" in AWS IAM in order to enforce least privilege access and prevent unintended or unauthorized access to resources.

ac.l2-3.1.2.title: Block public access to Amazon S3 buckets
ac.l2-3.1.2.description: Configure Amazon S3 to block public access to S3 buckets in order to protect data from unauthorized access.

ac.l2-3.1.2.title: Disable public access to Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) endpoints
ac.l2-3.1.2.description: Disable public access to Amazon EKS endpoints in order to avoid unintentional exposure and access to the cluster.

ac.l2-3.1.2.title: Manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to IAM identities
ac.l2-3.1.2.description: Manage access in AWS by creating and attaching IAM policies to ensure that only authorized individuals have access to data and resources.

ac.l2-3.1.2.title: Prevent IAM inline policies from using wildcard permissions in both Action and Resource elements
ac.l2-3.1.2.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from including 'Effect' 'Allow' with 'Action' '*' over 'Resource' '*' to enforce least privilege access.

ac.l2-3.1.2.title: Restrict IAM inline policies from using wildcard service actions
ac.l2-3.1.2.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from using 'Effect' 'Allow' with wildcard service actions to enforce least privilege access.

ac.l2-3.1.5.title: Manage access in AWS by creating policies and attaching them to IAM identities
ac.l2-3.1.5.description: Manage access in AWS by creating and attaching IAM policies to ensure that only authorized individuals have access to data and resources.

ac.l2-3.1.5.title: Prevent IAM inline policies from using wildcard permissions in both Action and Resource elements
ac.l2-3.1.5.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from including 'Effect' 'Allow' with 'Action' '*' over 'Resource' '*' to enforce least privilege access.

ac.l2-3.1.5.title: Restrict IAM inline policies from using wildcard service actions
ac.l2-3.1.5.description: Restrict IAM inline policies from using 'Effect' 'Allow' with wildcard service actions to enforce least privilege access.

ac.l2-3.1.8.title: Implement MFA for the AWS root account
ac.l2-3.1.8.description: Enable MFA for the root account to reduce brute-force and unauthorized access risk.

ac.l2-3.1.8.title: Enable hardware MFA for the root account
ac.l2-3.1.8.description: Enable hardware MFA for the root account to protect against unauthorized access.

ac.l2-3.1.8.title: Enforce account password policy for IAM users
ac.l2-3.1.8.description: Define strong password parameters within the AWS account password policy.

ac.l2-3.1.8.title: Enable MFA for IAM console users
ac.l2-3.1.8.description: Enable MFA for IAM users with console passwords to strengthen authentication.

ac.l2-3.1.9.title: Restrict and monitor the use of AWS root account
ac.l2-3.1.9.description: Restrict and monitor root user usage to support system use notification requirements.

ac.l2-3.1.10.title: Enable Amazon S3 bucket object lock
ac.l2-3.1.10.description: Enable object lock in S3 buckets to protect against unintentional changes or deletions.

ac.l2-3.1.10.title: Block public access to Amazon S3 buckets
ac.l2-3.1.10.description: Configure S3 to block public access to protect data from unauthorized access.

ac.l2-3.1.11.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
ac.l2-3.1.11.description: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners to manage session security.

ac.l2-3.1.11.title: Enable encryption for data in transit for DMS endpoints
ac.l2-3.1.11.description: Enable encryption for DMS endpoints to prevent unauthorized access during data transfer.

au.l2-3.3.1.title: Configure AWS CloudTrail trails to be multi-region
au.l2-3.3.1.description: Configure CloudTrail to support multi-Region trails in order to centralize log aggregation of all events for audit log reviews.

au.l2-3.3.1.title: Enable security trails in AWS CloudTrail
au.l2-3.3.1.description: Enable and create security trails in AWS CloudTrail to retain and protect audit logs of AWS API call activity.

au.l2-3.3.1.title: Enable log file validation for AWS CloudTrail logs
au.l2-3.3.1.description: Protect log files generated by CloudTrail against tampering or unauthorized modifications in order to support log file integrity and reliability.

au.l2-3.3.1.title: Configure AWS CloudTrail to send logs to Amazon CloudWatch Logs
au.l2-3.3.1.description: Configure CloudTrail to send logs to CloudWatch Logs in order to monitor activity trails and notify when potentially anomalous activity occurs.

au.l2-3.3.1.title: Enable encryption for AWS CloudTrail trails
au.l2-3.3.1.description: Encrypt all log data generated by CloudTrail while at rest to protect the confidentiality and integrity of the log data stored in CloudTrail.

au.l2-3.3.1.title: Enable Amazon S3 data event logging for read operations in AWS CloudTrail
au.l2-3.3.1.description: Configure AWS CloudTrail to log all Amazon S3 read data events using multi-Region trails for comprehensive auditing of S3 object-level read activities.

au.l2-3.3.2.title: Ensure Amazon CloudWatch alarms cannot be disabled
au.l2-3.3.2.description: Ensure Amazon CloudWatch alarms cannot be disabled to allow continuous monitoring and timely responses to critical workloads.

au.l2-3.3.2.title: Define retention periods for Amazon CloudWatch Log groups
au.l2-3.3.2.description: Define retention periods for CloudWatch Log groups to ensure that stored logs are retained for as long as the business requires.

au.l2-3.3.2.title: Enable AWS Security Hub service
au.l2-3.3.2.description: Activate Security Hub in the AWS management console and configure the service to receive findings from supported AWS security services.

au.l2-3.3.4.title: Ensure CloudWatch alarms cannot be disabled
au.l2-3.3.4.description: Verify CloudWatch alarm actions are enabled to support audit failure alerting.

au.l2-3.3.4.title: Verify CloudWatch alarm settings
au.l2-3.3.4.description: Verify CloudWatch alarm settings are properly configured for audit monitoring.

au.l2-3.3.4.title: Enable CloudTrail integration with CloudWatch Logs
au.l2-3.3.4.description: Send CloudTrail logs to CloudWatch for real-time audit failure monitoring and alerting.

au.l2-3.3.6.title: Configure CloudWatch log group retention period
au.l2-3.3.6.description: Set retention periods on CloudWatch log groups to support audit record management.

au.l2-3.3.6.title: Enable Amazon S3 bucket logging
au.l2-3.3.6.description: Enable S3 bucket logging to support audit record collection and analysis.

au.l2-3.3.7.title: Enable CloudTrail log file validation
au.l2-3.3.7.description: Enable log file validation to ensure integrity and timestamping of audit records.

au.l2-3.3.7.title: Enable CloudTrail integration with CloudWatch Logs
au.l2-3.3.7.description: Integrate CloudTrail with CloudWatch for time-synchronized audit record processing.

au.l2-3.3.8.title: Configure AWS CloudTrail logs in Amazon S3 buckets with no public access
au.l2-3.3.8.description: Configure the CloudTrail logs S3 bucket with no public access to maintain log integrity and confidentiality.

au.l2-3.3.8.title: Enforce retention policy for log archive in Amazon S3 buckets
au.l2-3.3.8.description: Prevents unauthorized modification of lifecycle configurations for Amazon S3 buckets, ensuring data retention policies remain intact.

au.l2-3.3.8.title: Prevent policy changes to log archive S3 buckets
au.l2-3.3.8.description: Blocks unauthorized modification of bucket policies for long-term archive Amazon S3 buckets to preserve the integrity of audit logs.

cm.l2-3.4.1.title: Backup Amazon Elastic Block Storage (EBS) volumes
cm.l2-3.4.1.description: Configure automatic backups for Amazon EBS volumes in order to prevent data loss and support recovery efforts in the event of a disruption.

cm.l2-3.4.1.title: Prevent unauthorized changes to AWS Config settings
cm.l2-3.4.1.description: Blocks all users and roles, except the AWS Control Tower service role, from modifying or disabling AWS Config settings across the organization.

cm.l2-3.4.1.title: Prevent unauthorized modifications to AWS Config Rules managed by Control Tower
cm.l2-3.4.1.description: Blocks all users and roles, except the AWS Control Tower service role, from modifying or deleting AWS Config rules and configuration aggregators.

cm.l2-3.4.1.title: Enable tagging for AWS AppConfig applications
cm.l2-3.4.1.description: Configure required tags for AWS AppConfig applications to support resource tracking, cost allocation, and access control.

cm.l2-3.4.3.title: Send event notifications from AWS CloudFormation stacks to an Amazon SNS topic
cm.l2-3.4.3.description: Send event notifications from CloudFormation stacks to an SNS topic to notify personnel of potential security events.

cm.l2-3.4.3.title: Prevent unauthorized changes to AWS Config settings
cm.l2-3.4.3.description: Blocks all users and roles, except the AWS Control Tower service role, from modifying or disabling AWS Config settings.

cm.l2-3.4.3.title: Prevent unauthorized modifications to AWS Config Rules managed by Control Tower
cm.l2-3.4.3.description: Blocks all users and roles, except the AWS Control Tower service role, from modifying or deleting AWS Config rules.

cm.l2-3.4.5.title: Restrict IAM policies with blacklisted actions
cm.l2-3.4.5.description: Restrict IAM policies to prevent unauthorized configuration changes.

cm.l2-3.4.5.title: Audit bucket policy changes prohibited
cm.l2-3.4.5.description: Prevent unauthorized changes to audit bucket policies to maintain configuration integrity.

cm.l2-3.4.5.title: Audit bucket retention policy
cm.l2-3.4.5.description: Enforce retention policies on audit buckets to protect configuration change records.

cm.l2-3.4.6.title: Disable automatic assignment of public IP addresses for Amazon VPC subnets
cm.l2-3.4.6.description: Disable automatic assignment of public IP addresses for Amazon VPC subnets to prevent instances from being accessed directly from the public Internet.

cm.l2-3.4.6.title: Restrict security group public Internet ingress traffic sources for remote access services
cm.l2-3.4.6.description: Prevent unauthorized access to EC2 resources by configuring security groups to restrict public Internet ingress traffic sources for remote access services.

cm.l2-3.4.6.title: Remove unused Amazon EC2 security groups
cm.l2-3.4.6.description: Review and remove unused EC2 security groups to reduce complexity in network management and improve network security.

cm.l2-3.4.9.title: Restrict IAM policies with blacklisted actions
cm.l2-3.4.9.description: Restrict IAM policies to control user-installed software.

cm.l2-3.4.9.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
cm.l2-3.4.9.description: Enable Security Hub to detect unauthorized software installations.

ia.l2-3.5.1.title: Enable MFA for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users that have a console password
ia.l2-3.5.1.description: Enable MFA for IAM users that have a console password in order to strengthen user authentication.

ia.l2-3.5.1.title: Configure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users
ia.l2-3.5.1.description: Configure MFA for IAM users to protect resources from unauthorized access.

ia.l2-3.5.1.title: Enable MFA for the AWS root account
ia.l2-3.5.1.description: Enable MFA for the root account to add an extra layer of protection for the most privileged account.

ia.l2-3.5.1.title: Remove unused secrets in AWS Secrets Manager
ia.l2-3.5.1.description: Remove unused secrets in Secrets Manager to revoke unnecessary access.

ia.l2-3.5.2.title: Enable MFA for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users that have a console password
ia.l2-3.5.2.description: Enable MFA for IAM users that have a console password in order to strengthen user authentication.

ia.l2-3.5.2.title: Configure Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) users
ia.l2-3.5.2.description: Configure MFA for IAM users to protect resources from unauthorized access.

ia.l2-3.5.2.title: Enable MFA for the AWS root account
ia.l2-3.5.2.description: Enable MFA for the root account to add an extra layer of protection for the most privileged account.

ia.l2-3.5.2.title: Remove unused secrets in AWS Secrets Manager
ia.l2-3.5.2.description: Remove unused secrets in Secrets Manager to revoke unnecessary access.

ia.l2-3.5.11.title: Enable MFA for IAM console users
ia.l2-3.5.11.description: Enable MFA for IAM console users to provide secure authentication feedback.

ia.l2-3.5.11.title: Enable MFA for all IAM users
ia.l2-3.5.11.description: Enable MFA for all IAM users to strengthen authentication mechanisms.

ir.l2-3.6.1.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
ir.l2-3.6.1.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection to support incident response training scenarios.

ir.l2-3.6.1.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ir.l2-3.6.1.description: Enable Security Hub to provide incident data for response training.

ir.l2-3.6.1.title: Enable Amazon GuardDuty for threat detection
ir.l2-3.6.1.description: Enable GuardDuty to continuously monitor for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior to protect AWS accounts, workloads, and data.

ir.l2-3.6.1.title: Enable AWS Security Hub for centralized security findings
ir.l2-3.6.1.description: Enable Security Hub to aggregate, organize, and prioritize security findings from multiple AWS services and third-party products.

ir.l2-3.6.1.title: Configure CloudWatch alarms for security events
ir.l2-3.6.1.description: Configure CloudWatch alarms to detect and alert on security-relevant events for rapid incident response.

ir.l2-3.6.3.title: Enable GuardDuty malware protection
ir.l2-3.6.3.description: Enable GuardDuty malware protection to support incident response testing.

ir.l2-3.6.3.title: Encrypt backup recovery points
ir.l2-3.6.3.description: Encrypt backup recovery points to support incident response and recovery testing.

ir.l2-3.6.3.title: Enable AWS Security Hub
ir.l2-3.6.3.description: Enable Security Hub to support incident response testing and validation.

mp.l2-3.8.9.title: Backup Amazon Elastic Block Storage (EBS) volumes
mp.l2-3.8.9.description: Configure automatic backups for Amazon EBS volumes in order to prevent data loss and support recovery efforts in the event of a disruption.

mp.l2-3.8.9.title: Configure AWS Backup Plan for Amazon S3 resources
mp.l2-3.8.9.description: Configure AWS Backup Plan for Amazon S3 resources to mitigate data loss and provide data protection.

mp.l2-3.8.9.title: Enable encryption at rest for AWS Backup recovery points
mp.l2-3.8.9.description: Enable encryption at rest for AWS Backup recovery points to reduce the risk of unauthorized access to data.

mp.l2-3.8.9.title: Configure recovery points for AWS Backup such that they do not expire before the retention period
mp.l2-3.8.9.description: Configure recovery points for AWS Backup such that they do not expire before the retention period in order to prevent data loss and meet retention requirements.

mp.l2-3.8.9.title: Ensure Amazon RDS database instances have automatic backups configured with retention period of at least 7 days
mp.l2-3.8.9.description: Require Amazon RDS database instances to have automated backups enabled with a backup retention period greater than or equal to seven days.

ra.l2-3.11.2.title: Enable Amazon Inspector for vulnerability scanning
ra.l2-3.11.2.description: Enable Inspector to automatically discover workloads and continuously scan for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure.

ra.l2-3.11.2.title: Enable ECR image scanning for container vulnerabilities
ra.l2-3.11.2.description: Enable ECR image scanning to identify software vulnerabilities in container images.

ra.l2-3.11.2.title: Configure Systems Manager for patch compliance
ra.l2-3.11.2.description: Use Systems Manager Patch Manager to automate patching and track patch compliance across EC2 instances.

sc.l2-3.13.1.title: Remove unused Amazon EC2 security groups
sc.l2-3.13.1.description: Review and remove unused EC2 security groups to reduce complexity in network management and improve network security and performance.

sc.l2-3.13.1.title: Do not use default security group rules in Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
sc.l2-3.13.1.description: Change VPC default security groups rules from their default configuration and ensure defaults are not used to prevent unrestricted inbound/outbound traffic.

sc.l2-3.13.1.title: Deploy firewalls managed through AWS Network firewalls across multiple Availability Zones
sc.l2-3.13.1.description: Deploy firewalls managed through AWS Network firewalls across multiple Availability Zones to provide redundancy, scalability and improved availability.

sc.l2-3.13.1.title: Define a default action for handling stateless, fragmented packets in AWS Network Firewall policies
sc.l2-3.13.1.description: Define a default action for handling stateless, fragmented packets in Network Firewall policies that match defined control parameters.

sc.l2-3.13.1.title: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancers
sc.l2-3.13.1.description: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancer to customize security settings according to specific requirements and specific cipher standards.

sc.l2-3.13.3.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
sc.l2-3.13.3.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to separate user functionality from system management.

sc.l2-3.13.3.title: Configure WAF web ACL rules
sc.l2-3.13.3.description: Configure WAF web ACLs to enforce separation of user and system functionality.

sc.l2-3.13.4.title: Close VPC default security groups
sc.l2-3.13.4.description: Close VPC default security groups to prevent information leakage in shared resources.

sc.l2-3.13.8.title: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancers
sc.l2-3.13.8.description: Configure custom TLS policies for listeners on AWS Elastic Load Balancer to customize security settings.

sc.l2-3.13.8.title: Encrypt Amazon OpenSearch Service node communications
sc.l2-3.13.8.description: Encrypt OpenSearch end-to-end node communication by using a secure protocol.

sc.l2-3.13.8.title: ELBv2 listeners must use HTTPS or TLS encryption for data in transit
sc.l2-3.13.8.description: Monitors Application Load Balancer and Network Load Balancer listeners to verify they are configured with HTTPS or TLS termination.

sc.l2-3.13.9.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
sc.l2-3.13.9.description: Configure TLS policies for ELB to manage network session disconnection.

sc.l2-3.13.9.title: Enable encryption for data in transit for DMS endpoints
sc.l2-3.13.9.description: Enable DMS endpoint encryption to manage secure network disconnection.

sc.l2-3.13.10.title: Prevent public access in AWS KMS key policies
sc.l2-3.13.10.description: Configure AWS KMS key policies to prohibit public access to encryption keys.

sc.l2-3.13.10.title: Ensure AWS KMS key policies limit grant creation to AWS services only
sc.l2-3.13.10.description: Restrict AWS KMS key policies to include statements that limit the creation of grants to AWS services only.

sc.l2-3.13.10.title: Restrict KMS grant creation to AWS services or AWS service principals only
sc.l2-3.13.10.description: Enforce that AWS KMS grants are created exclusively by or for AWS services.

sc.l2-3.13.11.title: Enable encryption for data in transit for Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) endpoint
sc.l2-3.13.11.description: Enable encryption for data in transit for Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) endpoint to prevent unintended information disclosure.

sc.l2-3.13.11.title: Enable encryption in transit for AWS AppSync API caches
sc.l2-3.13.11.description: Configure encryption in transit for AWS AppSync API caches to secure data transmission between services.

sc.l2-3.13.11.title: EMR Security Configuration Encryption at Rest Enabled
sc.l2-3.13.11.description: Monitors Amazon EMR security configurations to verify that encryption at rest is properly enabled.

sc.l2-3.13.12.title: Enable VPC flow logs
sc.l2-3.13.12.description: Enable VPC flow logs to monitor collaborative computing device network traffic.

sc.l2-3.13.12.title: Close VPC default security groups
sc.l2-3.13.12.description: Close default security groups to restrict collaborative computing device access.

sc.l2-3.13.13.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
sc.l2-3.13.13.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to control mobile code execution.

sc.l2-3.13.13.title: Configure WAF web ACL rules
sc.l2-3.13.13.description: Configure WAF web ACLs to filter and control mobile code.

sc.l2-3.13.15.title: Configure custom TLS policies for ELB listeners
sc.l2-3.13.15.description: Configure TLS policies for ELB to ensure session authenticity.

sc.l2-3.13.15.title: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups are not empty
sc.l2-3.13.15.description: Ensure WAFv2 rule groups contain rules to validate session authenticity.

sc.l2-3.13.16.title: Encrypt data at rest using AWS Key Management Service (KMS) in Amazon DynamoDB
sc.l2-3.13.16.description: Encrypt customer data at rest in Amazon DynamoDB using AWS KMS to maintain confidentiality and prevent unauthorized data exposure.

sc.l2-3.13.16.title: Prevent public access in AWS KMS key policies
sc.l2-3.13.16.description: Configure AWS KMS key policies to prohibit public access to encryption keys. This ensures that KMS keys remain private and accessible only to authorized entities.

sc.l2-3.13.16.title: Encrypt Amazon OpenSearch Service node communications
sc.l2-3.13.16.description: Encrypt OpenSearch end-to-end node communication by using a secure protocol to protect data from unauthorized disclosure.

sc.l2-3.13.16.title: Ensure Amazon RDS database clusters have encryption at rest configured
sc.l2-3.13.16.description: Enforce that Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) database clusters are configured with storage encryption at rest.

sc.l2-3.13.16.title: Ensure Amazon Kinesis data streams have encryption at rest configured
sc.l2-3.13.16.description: Enforce server-side encryption for Amazon Kinesis data streams to automatically encrypt data before it is written to storage.

si.l2-3.14.1.title: Automate patching with Systems Manager Patch Manager
si.l2-3.14.1.description: Use Systems Manager Patch Manager to automate the process of patching managed instances with security-related updates.

si.l2-3.14.1.title: Enable automatic minor version upgrades for RDS
si.l2-3.14.1.description: Enable automatic minor version upgrades for RDS database instances to ensure timely application of security patches.

si.l2-3.14.1.title: Use latest Lambda runtime versions
si.l2-3.14.1.description: Ensure Lambda functions use the latest runtime versions to benefit from security patches and improvements.

si.l2-3.14.2.title: Enable Malware Protection in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.2.description: Activate Malware Protection feature in Amazon GuardDuty to detect potential malware in your AWS environment.

si.l2-3.14.2.title: Enable Amazon GuardDuty in AWS account and region
si.l2-3.14.2.description: Implement intrusion detection (IDS) capabilities by enabling GuardDuty to monitor for and detect suspicious activity.

si.l2-3.14.2.title: Enable Amazon EKS Audit Log Monitoring in GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.2.description: Activate Amazon EKS Audit Log Monitoring feature in Amazon GuardDuty for enhanced security monitoring of Kubernetes clusters.

si.l2-3.14.4.title: Enable Malware Protection in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.4.description: Activate Malware Protection feature in Amazon GuardDuty to detect potential malware in your AWS environment.

si.l2-3.14.4.title: Enable Amazon GuardDuty in AWS account and region
si.l2-3.14.4.description: Implement intrusion detection (IDS) capabilities by enabling GuardDuty to monitor for and detect suspicious activity.

si.l2-3.14.4.title: Enable Amazon EKS Audit Log Monitoring in GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.4.description: Activate Amazon EKS Audit Log Monitoring feature in Amazon GuardDuty for enhanced security monitoring of Kubernetes clusters.

si.l2-3.14.5.title: Enable Malware Protection in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.5.description: Activate Malware Protection feature in Amazon GuardDuty to detect potential malware in your AWS environment.

si.l2-3.14.5.title: Enable Amazon GuardDuty in AWS account and region
si.l2-3.14.5.description: Implement intrusion detection (IDS) capabilities by enabling GuardDuty to monitor for and detect suspicious activity.

si.l2-3.14.5.title: Enable Amazon EKS Audit Log Monitoring in GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.5.description: Activate Amazon EKS Audit Log Monitoring feature in Amazon GuardDuty for enhanced security monitoring of Kubernetes clusters.

si.l2-3.14.6.title: Enable EKS Runtime Monitoring in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.6.description: Activate Amazon EKS Runtime Monitoring with automated agent management in GuardDuty to detect runtime security threats in Kubernetes clusters.

si.l2-3.14.6.title: Identify non-archived security findings in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.6.description: Identify non-archived security findings (low/medium/high severity) in Amazon GuardDuty that exceed the configured number of days.

si.l2-3.14.6.title: Enable Malware Protection in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.6.description: Activate Malware Protection feature in Amazon GuardDuty to detect potential malware in your AWS environment.

si.l2-3.14.7.title: Enable EKS Runtime Monitoring in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.7.description: Activate Amazon EKS Runtime Monitoring with automated agent management in GuardDuty to detect runtime security threats in Kubernetes clusters.

si.l2-3.14.7.title: Identify non-archived security findings in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.7.description: Identify non-archived security findings (low/medium/high severity) in Amazon GuardDuty that exceed the configured number of days.

si.l2-3.14.7.title: Enable Malware Protection in Amazon GuardDuty
si.l2-3.14.7.description: Activate Malware Protection feature in Amazon GuardDuty to detect potential malware in your AWS environment.
